sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.88 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: We say that an element$u$of a group$G$is a test element\/ if for any endomorphism$$of$G$the equality$(u)=u$implies that$$is an automorphism of~$G$. Does a free soluble group of rank$2$and derived length$d>2$have any test elements? { B.\,Fine, V.\,Shpilrain} Yes, it does (E.\,I.\,Timoshenko, Algebra and Logic, 45, no.\,4 (2006), 254--260.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
